content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible curving right, and the car appears to be on the left side of the track heading into this right curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 103, target = (103 + 5) % 120 = 108
- Waypoint 108: x=1.1987, y=1.2281
- Car: x=1.2062, y=1.3936
- Bearing = atan2(1.2281 - 1.3936, 1.1987 - 1.2062) = atan2(-0.1655, -0.0075) ≈ -87.4° (nearly straight down)
- Current heading: -47.16°
- Delta = -87.4° - (-47.16°) = -40.2° → right turn needed

Delta ≈ -40° suggests a significant right turn needed to follow the track curving south.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply right ahead (waypoint 108 delta ≈ -40°), car is left of center heading south-southeast, need significant right steering to follow the track line downward.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track loops sharply south around waypoints 103-112 then curves back northeast",
    "At progress ~87%, car is in the bottom-right section curving south then east",
    "Waypoints 108-112 trend south/southeast — car must steer right significantly here",
    "Car heading -47° needs to decrease further (more negative) to follow track south",
    "Positive delta = steer left, negative delta = steer right",
    "At delta -40°, use steering_angle=-20 at speed 1.0",
    "Car is left of center — track curving right confirms right steering needed",
    "Previous -15° steering at delta -28° was correct but track is curving more now",
    "After waypoint 112, track begins heading northeast — watch for left turn",
    "Avoid oversteer oscillation on straights; use smallest valid angle when delta < 10°"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.115, 1.160, 1.206],
    "y": [1.446, 1.420, 1.394],
    "yaw": [-54.3, -50.5, -47.2]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -40.2,
  "speed": 1.0,
  "steering_angle": -20.0
}
```
